entry.title: Re: Unable to build copied app
entry.content:
Hello, thank you for reaching out to us. Could you please try saving it as a new project instead of copying? When you copy a project, the backend needs to be manually redeployed to be accessible as an integration.

here are the more details - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/save-as-new-project?locale=en-US&q=copying%20apps

entry.title: Re: when I Generate ABAP repository objects.error : Field...
entry.content:
Try to replace the datatype of the field

"createdby" with the dataelement "abp_creation_user" in your database table.

entry.title: Re: How to automate the headers in SAP Disclosure Management
entry.content:
Hello @Srini971 , it's not possible to refresh DM datalink directly in header and footer, however you can use Word bookmark as a workaround. To do that insert named Word bookmark into the header and assign same name to DM floating field with an empty space character behind it - so that once you refresh DM link with the name of the entity required, it will copy over to header.

However, unless you have dozens and dozens of entities, I would rather suggest updating header directly in the report template, it's a quick thing to do.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: @Shilpa_Shankar Thanks. It's the searching by text that I've struggled with. For textArrangement: #SEPARATE the search help is by technical key, so how to attach the search to the text field. Setting the textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY shows Status as Text but removes the Technical value from the list:

entry.title: Re: Data is incomplete check message No:F2125
entry.content:
Data is incomplete check message No:F2125(Partner function error)-

SAP SD ERROR TUTORIALS-

"No: F2125 - Partner function error" in SAP usually indicates that there is an issue with the partner function assignment in your document or master data. Partner functions define the roles that business partners play in various transactions, such as a customer being a sold-to party, ship-to party, payer, or bill-to party.

FOLLOW STEPS-

Solution-https://www.learntosap.com/mmerrorpage16.html

 

 

Data is incomplete check message No:F2125(Partner function error)-www.learntosap.com

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I’m Ahmed, and I’m really excited about the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. With my background in accounting and over 2.5 years as an ERP consultant in the industry, I’ve developed a strong interest in blending technology with business. I’m currently pursuing my ACCA qualification and am eager to transition into a career in SAP, especially as I’m looking to switch careers mid-life.

This program seems like the perfect fit for where I’m headed, and I believe the training and certification it offers will be key in making this career shift successful.

I believe I qualify under the following categories:

  • People looking to switch careers mid-life
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may not have access to the same economic opportunities as those in urban or more privileged areas

I’m looking forward to being a part of this journey and contributing to the SAP community. Thank you for considering my application!

Best regards,
Ahmed

entry.title: CDS view field date element alpha routine
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a field that displays a document number. For some kinds of document type, document number should be display as it is in the DB, while for some kinds of document type, document number should remove leading zeroes and this can be done by alpha routine in data element.

My question is, can I use different date elements casting against the document type (CASE WHEN)? I tried but looks like only the first date element is working.

Anyone could give some help here?

Regards,

Ming

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm wondering if there's any way to adjust the user that the background process will be run against. Let's say that a casual user performs an action, and that action must kick off a sophisticated process in the background that the casual user will not authorization to perform.

This would be for Steampunk.

Thanks!

entry.title: Cockpit SAP 7
entry.content:
Dear all, 

As you know, the e-invoice system is implemented in Romania. The company where I'm working has also implemented the Cockpit transaction in SAP, for suplliers , customer end S-fapt. We would like to download the pdf and the XML files created from the eDocument Cockpit app( after the transmission in SPV ) - for a specific period (say the last week)  in order to save them locally. Is this possible/forseen?

How we can save export multiple pdf ( but the E-factura invoice ) and the saving should be done individually, with name and invoice number ? it is not normal to enter each individual invoice( supllier sau customer ) and save it manually... or to the customers after it is sent and authorized by the ANAF , to be automatically downloaded in a signed pdf and xml folder

in the pictures above, it can be seen that the customer's name does not exist. It is possible to add ?What about the supplier? there is no number, there is no VAT (CUI ), name...can it be added?
#E-invoice #Copckpit #export #multiple

entry.title: Mastering ABAP: Unveiling the Power of OOP Concepts of Class & object
entry.content:
Object oriented programing helps in organizing and structuring code in a more manageable way.

Real Time example about OOP


Car is a class. It defines general features like wheels, doors, and methods like start() and stop().A specific car, like a red Toyota Corolla, is an object of this class. Engine is abstracted. You don’t see how it works, just how to start the car. This hides complexity and shows only what’s necessary. The Dashboard encapsulates all controls (speedometer, fuel gauge) in one place, similar to how a class encapsulates data and methods. ElectricCar inherits from the Car class. It has all the features of a car but adds new ones, like a battery. Driving modes like Sport or Eco mode allow the same car to behave differently based on the mode, illustrating how methods can behave differently in different contexts.

 

CLASS AND OBJECT — defines the properties (attributes) and behaviors (methods) that the objects created from the class will have. An object, on the other hand, is an instance of a class that contains actual values for the attributes defined in the class.

Real Time Example

The class represents a group of objects having similar properties and behavior. For example, the animal type Dog is a class while a particular dog named Coffee is an object of the Dog class.

SYNTAX for creating Class

 

 

CLASS <class_name> DEFINITION.
  [PUBLIC|PROTECTED|PRIVATE] SECTION.
    
    DATA: <attribute_name> TYPE <data_type>,
          ...
    METHODS: <method_name> ,
             ...
ENDCLASS.

CLASS <class_name> IMPLEMENTATION.
  "Define methods here
  METHOD <method_name>.
    "Method implementation code
  ENDMETHOD.
  ...
ENDCLASS.

 

 

<class_name>: Name of the class you are creating.
SECTION: Specifies the visibility of the class components (attributes, methods):
PUBLIC SECTION: Accessible from outside the class.
PROTECTED SECTION: Accessible within the class and subclasses.
PRIVATE SECTION: Accessible only within the class itself.
DATA: Used to define attributes (variables) within the class.
METHODS: Used to declare methods (functions) that can be performed by objects of the class.
METHOD: This block is where you implement the logic of the method defined in the class.

 

DATA: <object_name> TYPE REF TO <class_name>.
CREATE OBJECT <object_name>.

 

SYNTAX for creating Object

<object_name>: The name of the object (reference variable) you are creating.
TYPE REF TO <class_name>: Specifies that <object_name> is a reference to an object of type <class_name>.
CREATE OBJECT: This statement instantiates the object.

 

CLASS DEMO1 DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
  METHODS :
  M1.
  DATA: NAME TYPE STRING.
 ENDCLASS.

 CLASS DEMO1 IMPLEMENTATION.
   METHOD M1.
   WRITE 😕 'NAME :', NAME.
   ENDMETHOD.

  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS STATIC DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
  CLASS-DATA : COUNTER TYPE I.
  CLASS-METHODS : COUNT.
  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS STATIC IMPLEMENTATION.
       METHOD COUNT.
     COUNTER += 1.
     WRITE : / COUNTER.
    ENDMETHOD.
    ENDCLASS.

  START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: OBJ1 TYPE REF TO DEMO1.
  CREATE OBJECT OBJ1.
  OBJ1->NAME = 'NILA'.
  OBJ1->M1( ).
 STATIC=>COUNT( ).

 

Class DEMO1
Definition:

This class has a method M1 and a data attribute NAME.
The method M1 is declared in the PUBLIC SECTION, meaning it can be accessed from outside the class.
The data attribute NAME is of type STRING, also declared in the PUBLIC SECTION.
Implementation:

The method M1 is implemented to write the text “NAME:” followed by the value of the NAME attribute to the output.
Class STATIC
Definition:

This class has a class-level attribute COUNTER and a class method COUNT.
CLASS-DATA COUNTER: This is a static variable, shared across all instances of the class.
CLASS-METHODS COUNT: This is a static method, which can be called without creating an instance of the class.
Implementation:

The method COUNT increments the COUNTER by 1 and then writes its value to the output.
Main Program (START-OF-SELECTION)
Creating Object OBJ1:

An object OBJ1 of type DEMO1 is created.
The NAME attribute of OBJ1 is set to “NILA”.
The M1 method of OBJ1 is called, which prints “NAME: NILA” to the output.
Calling Static Method COUNT:

The static method COUNT of class STATIC is called directly without creating an object.
This increments the COUNTER by 1 and prints its value.

 

IMPORTANT POINTS TO NOTE

Instance Method vs. Static Method:
M1 is an instance method, meaning it operates on a specific object (OBJ1).
COUNT is a static method, meaning it can be called on the class itself (STATIC=>COUNT( )).
Instance Attribute vs. Static Attribute:
NAME is an instance attribute, meaning each object of DEMO1 has its own NAME.
COUNTER is a static attribute, meaning it is shared by all instances of the STATIC class (though in this example, no instances are created, and it’s accessed directly via the class).

entry.title: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
Cause i have a task to download document from portal, and there is a specific key ( dongle or hardware key, i don't know how it's called in English ) which allows to enter portal and download documents, so i need to start programm on a specific computer with this dongle.

entry.title: Re: OPEN DATASET syntax for Unicode and non-Unicode, mixe...
entry.content: Unicode

entry.title: Re: OPEN DATASET syntax for Unicode and non-Unicode, mixe...
entry.content:
Hi,

this is a general problem when exchanging char-like data between Non-Unicode and Unicode based systems. It can happen during transport, interfacing (e.g. via RFC) or (as in your case) file exchange.

 Unicode is supporting more than 100000 characters, while code page 1100 (ISO-Latin-1) supports less than 256. As soon as a character outside the Non-Unicode codepage is transferred, the system needs raise an error or to replace it with a replacement character (default in SAP is "#") Alternatively it might be also possible (as already mentioned) to convert the problematic character via transliteration.

However when transferring textual data from Unicode to Non-Unicode, you cannot avoid risk of data loss - this will always be recognized (at the latest) in the receiving Non-Unicode system.

By the way using binary mode is not an option at all - the content will not be readable anymore ...

The best solution would be to use Unicode systems in the landscape only, but this is often not possible in reality ....

Please also have a look at SAP note 1322715 and the attachment of that SAP note called  Requirements_of_ABAP_Programs_in_Unicode_Systems.pdf.

Best regards,

Nils

entry.title: Re: Issue with long running PAL Function on HANA
entry.content: In that case, the problem apparently lies within the procedure itself and it is not related to the BW DTP/TRFN framework. I am from BW so we need to wait for someone with experience in this specific HANA procedure.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi,

If you are using BTP Trial system, you cannot add SAP_BR_Developer. Kindly delete the instance and run the Booster to set up ABAP Environment. Download the service key and use it to create a new ABAP Cloud Project. If you remember your package you can add it to the favorites else create a new one. I also noticed that BTP Trial system has less objects than it had earlier. Probably it has been refreshed.

entry.title: Re: How to get the HTML code of a website with SAP Build...
entry.content: Hallo, Ziel des ganzen ist es am Ende auch gar nicht die Website aufzuzeichnen, sondern in einen Prozess die URL einer Website einzugeben und dann den HTML code von der gesamten Website zurückzubekommen. Danach soll dieser dann nach bestimmten Werten durchsucht werden.

entry.title: Re: Experience with Deleting Company Codes Before Go-Live
entry.content: Organizational Structure is unforgiving. In the 2SL once created you cannot delete company codes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Copy Data to speci...
entry.content:
Hi @guenay,

you need to write your script like follows:

DATA([d/DIMENSION] = [d/DIMENSION].[p/PROPERTY]) 
= RESULTLOOKUP()

I hope this helps
Best regards
Susanne

Edu

entry.title: XSA Service Instances Migration from On-premise to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
In the last blog, you learned migration from an on-premise SAP HANA database to an SAP HANA Cloud database instance using the Self-Service Migration for SAP HANA Cloud. Now, will see on how to migrate the XS advanced service instances from on-premise to Cloud Foundry.

Figure: Migration Landscape

Pre-requisites

  • Connectivity Service Entitlement with the service plan “connectivity_proxy” must be enabled for your subaccount using the SAP BTP cockpit.
  • Supported for SAP HANA Database Revision 53 or greater.
  • Source and Tenant databases has XS advanced installed.
  • Migration user in the XSA tenant database of source system with necessary privileges.
  • Establish a connection to the XSA tenant database of source system using Cloud Connector.

Note: Services Instances in default SAP space is not supported for migration.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud Central, navigate to “Migrations”.
  2. Choose “Create Migration”, specify the migration information and then “Create”.
  3. In the PLAN phase,
  • Enter the virtual host and port that you specified in the cloud connector configuration.
  • Enable Cloud Connector and specify the Cloud Connector Location ID.
  • Enter the credentials of the migration user that was created in the XSA tenant database of the source system.
  • Check Compatibility with SAP HANA Cloud.

  • Tool runs a series of database and application compatibility checks. Once the Self-Service Migration tool finishes checking your database, it lists any incompatibilities found. 
  • Only checks with the severity 'CRITICAL' and 'UNKNOWN' will forbid the migration. Checks with the severity 'ERROR' or below will allow the migration; however, it is possible that some functionality may not work as expected in the target database.
  • Review and resolve the incompatibilities where possible. Choose Proceed to Preparation Phase.

    4. In the PREPARATION phase, provide the following information:

       Migrate XS advanced service instances (SAP HANA Schemas and HDI containers) to Cloud Foundry.

     As the source database has XS advanced installed and the tenant database selected for migration is enabled for XS advanced, the connection to the XS advanced database is configured through the SAP Cloud Connector.

  • Provide the login credentials of the XS advanced administrator user.
  • Configure the connection to the XS advanced runtime through Cloud Connector.
  • Choose Configure Endpoint to generate a configuration file that you  have to upload to SAP Cloud Connector in a later step.

  • Download the Configuration file and upload the same in Cloud Connector. Refer to point 9 in the link for more details.

  • Click on Save and Proceed.

  • Specify the credentials of the SAP BTP technical user.

  • Map the source and target spaces which contain the service instances to be migrated.

  • Specify the credentials of the migration user and Choose Save and Proceed to Select Target.

  • Choose the target database for the migration and enter the credentials of the DBADMIN user on the target database.

  • Confirm all the migration choices and Choose Continue to Execution Phase to proceed to the next phase of the migration.

     5. In the EXECUTION phase, choose “Start Migration” to start the migration process and monitor progress in the status bars. After the migration, a summary is displayed, and you can proceed to the VALIDATION phase.

    6. In the VALIDATION phase, the metadata and data of the migrated content are checked for consistency, also the record count of the tables to be migrated can be checked. Choose “Proceed to Finalization Phase”.

    7. In the FINALIZATION phase, the finalization process removes all artifacts that were temporarily stored by the migration service.Choose “Deactivate the migration user” and “Finalize Migration” to complete the migration and start the post-migration clean-up.

   8. The XS service instances is migrated to Cloud Foundry. A summary screen is displayed, containing information about the completed migration, including statistics about the migrated database, migration step durations, and validation statuses.

   9. “Clean Up Process” will archive the completed migration. Once the migration is archived, it serves as a reference and cannot be altered. Up to 20 migrations can be archived.

 

Hope this blog has helped! For more information refer SAP documentation SAP HANA Cloud Migration Guide.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: My Solution for Task 3:

entry.title: Crystal Reports take up to 23 sec to show PDF SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi All, 

we are facing the Issue by showing PDF of Documents which are based on CR Reports that these take up to 23 seconds untill the PDF is fully shown. 
The CR Reports are very simple and are just an adjusted Version of the Standard SAP B1 CR Reports, they do not contain any extra complicated queries or so.

meanwhile Documents which are based on PLD report are shown within 2-3 Seconds. 

its a HANA Installation and the SAP Version is 2208

does anyone have this issue? or a solution maybe?

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Thank you, Franzisca, for your reply. According to our business team, this was functioning in previous versions, but on my end, it hasn’t worked since I updated to the latest version of FSM. Regarding your question about how we’re trying to use and add the keyboard to FSM, we’re following the usual method: installing the third-party keyboard app and adding it to the list of iOS keyboards via Settings > Keyboard > Add New Keyboard.

entry.title: Issue with SMS Configuration in Solman
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently receiving alerts via email in Solution Manager, and to enhance the visibility of our systems, we have decided to implement SMS alerts as well. We have configured the SMS settings using our local provider by creating a web service. However, we have encountered an issue: although we can successfully trigger SMS messages manually from SBWP as a test, the SMS messages do not get sent in case of an actual alert.

Could you please provide us with guidance or documentation on how to configure the SMS alerts end-to-end in Solution Manager? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with SMS Configuration in Solman
entry.content: Please review KBA 2489425 - System Monitoring configuration to Send SMS / Text message alerts which provides all required steps to enable System Monitoring notifications via SMS.

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset Purchase IN S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Refer to the below link for material purchases with serial number. I think asset one should also work in similar way if material master record exists.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/900eb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

Let me know if you are unable to see the field.

Best Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Error occurred during creation of purchase requisitio...
entry.content:
As the error says: "Complete all required entry fields" - maybe best to check by creating an PR manually in ME51N and see which fields are mandatory to fill in your set up and see what is missing.

Furthermore maybe also good to check why you get a message of class /PLMU/UI_FRW in the first place when you are creating a production order in the back end via CO01. (Or compare production order creation in back-end vs via launchpad)

According to our business team, this was functioning in previous versions, but on my end, it hasn’t worked since I updated to the latest version of FSM. Regarding your question about how we’re trying to use and add the keyboard to FSM, we’re following the usual method: installing the third-party keyboard app and adding it to the list of iOS keyboards via Settings > Keyboard > Add New Keyboard.

entry.title: Re: Error occurred during creation of purchase requisitio...
entry.content: This note mentions error message CO876 /ME066. It is not related to /PLMU/UI_FRW 038 ("Complete all required entry fields"))

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content:
Hi Laurent,

Could you please share the link to the case you opened: 835635/2024 - "Not possible to add a third-party keyboard in the FSM Mobile App"?

We're still experiencing the same issue, and I'd like to follow up on the discussion with the FSM technical team.

entry.title: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
Hello Community! 

Is it possible we haven't started a discussion yet about your favorite books, or the "book that changed your life," or the type of books you enjoy reading⁉️

I know it's impossible to pick just one favorite book, but I'm looking for recommendations for my next read.

Right now, there's a book that changed my life, but after reading it three times, the third version of myself might not recommend it as highly, haha 😂

Your Erroneous ZonesYour Erroneous Zone

This book helped me take more responsibility for my choices and stop blaming external environments or situations.

I usually alternate between reading classic books and "self-help" books. 
One of my favorite classics is:

1985 - George Orwell

I don’t think this book needs much explanation. If you haven’t read it, you definitely should. It's an incredible book. I read it in my native language because it was too difficult for me to read in English.

But my favorite author overall, the one who got me into reading, is:

Herman Hessen

I love how he blends psychology with narrative. Siddhartha, Demian, Narcissus and Goldmund—I love them all. 

Special mention to another author:

Yuval Noah Harari

He wrote best-selling books that introduced me to history (which used to be one of my least favorite subjects in school 😅). Well written and a pleasure to read

Anyway, enough about me—I’m really curious to hear about your favorite books and authors.  I’m always on the lookout for something new to read, so hit me up with your suggestions!
What books have made a difference in your life, or just ones you really enjoyed?

Can’t wait to see what everyone’s into!

entry.title: Re: crowd partner admin
entry.content: Please delete that user. Then log in to the Crowd Workforce and send an invitation to that person. if you follow standard way user set up will appear correctly.

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content: I'm seeing few people posting blog posts as a discussion or as a question, probably because "Create a Blog" is disabled for new joiners, as @myenilmez noticed previously, is it possible to add a text to explain people what to do. I suggest something like your own phrase "new members who wish to blog will need to take this tutorial to receive blogging permissions".

entry.title: Focused Run Post Installation
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We recently installed Focused Run system in our environment. As per the master guide, we are not sure about Post steps.

Can we get documentation or steps for Post process. Like do we need get connect our system to cloud ALM for applications to work?

Also when we run T code : FRUN, not able to see any Tiles on the page.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Focused Run Post Installation
entry.content:
The Master Guide also has information on post installation activities (from section 4.7 - guide for FRUN 4.0 FP03 (latest)).

I recommend completing these steps if you have not already. However to supplement these steps, the Expert Portal provides additional information on application side step ups, supported products and also preparing the infrastructure post installation (see heading "Infrastructure Administration")

If you want to do monitoring of some Cloud Services, then you will need to connect FRUN to Cloud ALM. See heading "Monitoring Cloud Services through SAP Cloud ALM" in the same link above.

Finally, in regard to the Fiori Tiles. This could be for a number of reasons:

  • Permissions: https://help.sap.com/docs/FRUN/6ba510f3be2945e19e497bfb1065b022/3f2ab0ec535d4143826718864e96898b.html?q=fiori
  • Use of HTTPS/Web dispatcher: 3434823 - SAP Fiori Launchpad empty after system update 
  • Known issues:
    • 2423019 - Cannot find an app's tile in Fiori Launchpad - SAP S/4HANA
    • 2782173 - Tile missing on Fiori Launchpad

I recommend also checking latest release SAP Notes for your FP version in order to ensure an known issues can be addressed.

entry.title: Re: Transport Custom Workflow from development to quality...
entry.content:
Thanks so much for the help! As I can see the task is included in the transport request
TKEK902171 GXXXXXXX O2D_InquiryCreation_EmailNotification_Workflow_V3
TKEK9021XX GXXXXXXX Development/Correction Object List of Request Comment Entry: Released
      TKEK9021XX 20240823 134518 GEOXXXXX
              Standard Task (!!!! -->) 99800011
                  Workflow template 99800008

But for some reason it is not in the workflow builder in the quality server when I view the template in swdd.
Maybe this has to do something with the automatic workflow customizing? (swu3?) I am providing the screenshot with the settings

I am providing a screenshot

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to install sap Fiori on s/4 hana 2023 fps 01 on e...
entry.content:
You need to run the Maintenance Planner APP here, choose the particular system and plan a maintenance for it.

Choose to add an addon and search for FIORI. Remember that the FIORI version needs to match (be exactly the same) as the S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Selecting fields from ProductTreeLines inside a Produ...
entry.content:
Hi araob,

Can you try something as below and share the results with us?

 

{
    "QueryPath": "$crossjoin(ProductTrees,ProductTrees/ProductTreeLines)",
    "QueryOption": "$expand=ProductTrees($select=TreeCode, TreeType),ProductTrees/ProductTreeLines($select=ItemCode,ChildNum)&$filter=ProductTrees/TreeCode eq ProductTrees/ProductTreeLines/ParentItem"
}

 

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Custom changes in GGB1 doesn't carry over or apply
entry.content: You forgot to say if you have debugged your exit to make sure it's called and has changed BSEG-FDTAG? You didn't attach your code; how did you declare BSEG? What is your type of exit? (header, item, both?) Are you using your code in different clients? (requires code regeneration) Anyway, I looked at the forum and people saw that substitution cannot change FDTAG and they opted for another solution. By the way, why repeating the same question again? (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/a-standard-function-module-is-overwriting-my-logic-in-ggb1/qaq-p/13792895)

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Hi Franzisca, it doesn't work means that we can add the keyboard in our general settings. We can use it in the 'notes' app for example, so the keyboard works. Only when we open FSM mobile and we go into a checklist for example, we cannot choose that specific keyboard and we cannot add it.

entry.title: Re: sortProperty and filterProperty not working, but colu...
entry.content:
check the generated annotation file at runtime. it is probably disabled there.

if the length is exceeding certain size, the field is disabled for filtering in the generated annotation.

entry.title: Re: REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY + expand/collapse
entry.content: There is no standard feature to expand/collapse in the ALV Grid. If you want to develop this feature yourself, then the question is how to add a button (whatever it is) inside the ALV Grid. This question was already asked and answered. Here for instance: Solved: Buttons in fields of Grid ALV, but without a hotsp... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Index error in Design Console of SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Sija,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Could you please check if you are repeating the index naming? If possible, kindly attach your project so that we can cross-check the details more details.

entry.title: Re: SEVT TABLE DELETING OLD DATA
entry.content:
Hi LuisGuilherme 

There was may be some issue before, As if event sender is working fine it should automatically disappear 10 records every 3 second.If records are not moving from SEVT then there must be some issue.

The table "SEVT" contains the log of all steps or changes made within the SAP Business One, that is, each time you update a record either a document definitions, master data or user tables in this table creates a log of that change, but just who did it, when he did as object code or registration was made.

Content of the SEVT table can be truncated if you are not requiring to have the generated B1 events handled(If the integration framework is not using any scenarios, then we can completely deactivate the events creation in the B1 environment).We always recommend to create a backup On SBOCOMMON database before executing the deletion task.

If the SEVT table is having many entries, then it’s indicating as the Event Sender Configuration is not correct. Once the integration framework is installed, it enables the event collection via the table SEVT, and if those are not handled correctly by EventSender, then this is leading to the reported issue (huge records in SEVT table). If the EventSender is stopped, then it would not solve anything, the event will be generated.

Please configure the EventSender properly. If you will encounter some issue, please refer to the SAP note 1882000 as well.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Memoirs from The League of Storytellers
entry.content:
It is March 2024, and a motley crew of SAP colleagues are brought together by the League of Storytellers program; a thrilling learning adventure that promised to empower them with the skills and knowledge to become storytellers for SAP. This includes the tools necessary to host tours in the SAP Discovery Showroom, that is a physical, digital, and virtual multimedia experience for employees, customers, and partners.  Fast forward to 6 months later and the group is brought back together to celebrate the end of their rotation and reminisce about the journey they have been on. Here are the thoughts of four members, who share their experiences and some nuggets of wisdom: 

Hanna Tsatsopitas 

Working Student, Discover SAP 

Hanna TsatsopitasI learned a lot of new topics and content which I was not as experienced in before. I also gained presentation skills through the enablement sessions. I am typically a shy person, and I challenged myself to speak before an audience, which I enjoy. 

At the start of the program, the learning material and enablement sessions can seem like a daunting task. Do not be intimidated. You do not have to know everything by heart. It is important to ask questions to a buddy you will be paired with, as well as reaching out to others. Be present at the enablement sessions because they are helpful. In summary, great insights, clarity and presentation skills. 

 

Jennifer SpannagelJennifer Spannagel

Internal IT, Business Operations.  

My daily job involves making sure everyone SAP colleague understands the digital workplace. My role involves both marketing and communication explaining the nuts and bolts of the digital workplace. Ideally, my team creates material like blogs, newsletters, videos and training to bring out the best work in every colleague. 

I got in touch with the League of Storytellers during the Experience Technology open house in February 2024. I believed it was a good opportunity to expand my network and to learn more about what Corporate Processes and IT does at SAP. I was curious and wanted to get in touch with experts from the other modules and units, just as I am an expert in the digital workplace and how we work at SAP. I also wanted to advance my public speaking skills, because I had started exploring moderating and hosting roles. I wanted to stretch my boundaries and do something outside my comfort zone. 

I enjoyed the enablement sessions. We had a very nice group and comprehensive onboarding by Britta Lehn and Anja Rosker and their team. We got a good grasp of the important skills to put in use when giving tours in the showroom to different audiences: Customers, partners, colleagues. I also enjoyed being part of a versatile group of colleagues from different backgrounds.  I also enjoyed being part of an on-site, after years of working remotely since the pandemic.  

Take a chance and try. The SAP Discovery Showroom feels like a lounge with that homely, intimate atmosphere. And once you get into the program, embrace it, be present, bring the best of yourself and take the best of others to expand your knowledge.  

 

Michael Schmidt 

S2P (Source to Pay) / Strategic Procurement 

I have been at SAP for 26 years. In my earlier roles, I did some consulting work and training in the field, and then I moved to internal IT, working within the procurement area. 

Part of the reason I registered for the program was that I like to interact with people. I did a lot of customer training and workshops in the past. Being an instructor who speaks in front of people, you need to prepare and to extend your view, looking beyond your own nose. So, this program was an interesting way for me to do something different. 

I liked the program. I think it was good and well organized. I would say that owing to experience, I knew the material, but it was a good foundation. I could check the content and contribute to it as well.  

To any hopeful future League members, if you are interested in networking and extending your knowledge, or simply connecting to people, give it a try. I got in touch with interesting colleagues, which was great for me. 

Christian Philipp Walter 

Working Student, Experience Garage. 

Christian Philipp WalterI considered what the program would be for me before enrolling; I thought it would be a nice challenge and would help me build some invaluable public speaking experience. I wanted to experience talking to people and audiences and to get more comfortable at it.  

The enablement sessions were well-structured. I had a buddy I could call and ask questions. I received a good brief every time, and I understood how storytelling works. The enablement covered an extensive list topics, including soft skills, presentation skills and public speaking etiquette. My first presentation in the showroom was fun. I met different people and gave them an experience of how things work at SAP, from my point of view. Personally, I liked the module about Future-proofing SAP because of my work at Experience Garage; my team’s focus is people driven innovation. The topic felt closer to home. 

My advice? Go ahead, it is the best thing you can do for yourself, and the experience is great for your career and daily tasks.

entry.title: Re: Cannot see the SAP companion icon in SuccessFactors L...
entry.content:
Hi Shane, hi Ankur,

I activated SAP Companion and hereafter are the results of my findings:

- There is no integration between SAP Companion WA and LMS Administrators

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/wa-sap-companion-in-successfactors-learning-admin/qaq-p/12579675

- It works on the front-end, the question mark appears next to the Homepage search bar

- There is a know limitation for SuccessFactors: For SAP SuccessFactors, tutorials for the learning section are available as standard SAP content. Context help and guided tours are not available. (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ENABLE_NOW/9f37f9b5f4c84377bda071229c3fe51a/47f193df6dac1014be77df438d4899ec.html?locale=en-US)

Very curious to follow-up on that topic, would be a great added-value for both administrators and end users.

Kind regards,

Esther

The Servant - A simple story about the true essence of leadership
from JAMES C. HUNTER

entry.title: How to read XML string passed as input parameter to HANA stored procedure ?
entry.content:
I am trying to read a XML string passed as input parameter to a stored procedure and convert the values in the tags to a table variable. I am able to successfully parse XML to table if I use hardcoded string as below but I am not able to figure out how to supply input parameter XMLSTRING to inbuilt XMLTABLE() function.

Please help.

https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-sql-reference-guide/xmltable-function-string

 

PROCEDURE "sp_timesheetdata"(
    IN  XMLSTRING VARCHAR(5000)
)
   LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
   SQL SECURITY INVOKER
   --DEFAULT SCHEMA <default_schema_name>
   READS SQL DATA AS
BEGIN
DECLARE tempTable TABLE(ID VARCHAR(50), END_DATE VARCHAR(50), WORKORDER VARCHAR(50));

INSERT INTO :tempTable(ID,END_DATE,WORKORDER)
SELECT ID,END_DATE,WORKORDER FROM
XMLTABLE('resultset/row' PASSING  '<resultset>
<row>
<ID>12345</ID>
<DATE>2024-08-30 00:00:00</DATE>
<ORDER>273498</ORDER>
</row>
  <row>
  <ID>56789</ID>
  <DATE>2024-06-21 00:00:00</DATE>
  <ORDER>60657</ORDER>
  </row>
</resultset>'
   COLUMNS
   ID VARCHAR(50) PATH 'ID',
   DATE VARCHAR(50) PATH 'DATE',
   ORDER VARCHAR(50) PATH 'ORDER'
   ) as XTABLE;

   SELECT * FROM :tempTable; -- used in joins further

--
--

How to refer :XMLSTRING input parameter after keyword PASSING above ?

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Execution of Services (aka "Dual Order")
entry.content:
@Henrik_Kiessler

Hi Henrik,
excellent stuff.

Just having one question since you wrote "On the Executing Order all billing types and supported revenue recognition methods are possible".
Does this mean that in fact in the executing company a resource-related billing (DIP) is possible when there an execution order item / maintenance order comes into play?
If so, this would mean that dynamic costs can be charged by the executing company to the commercial
company. There the fixed-price item can only be charged to the customer via BDR. In real life that fixed price could be changed when necessary.

Is my understanding correct?

Thanks and BR
Harald

entry.title: Re: Length Of Service /Age Story Reports
entry.content:
Hi!
Great article! However, I can't seem to get the accurate age when using your calculation (and SAP's calculations based on KBAs). It's always rounding up the age. So, someone born in January 1994 will be calculated to 41 years old, but the person is only 40.

The KBA suggested to also have the ROUND in the beginning of the calculation, so by removing it and using your calculation I though it would do the trick - but it's still rounding up.

Do you have any suggestions?

entry.title: Special character # -issue in the file generated
entry.content:
hi All,

We are using SAP Integration Suite for converting the input XML file into a fixed-length flat file and placing it in the AL11 directory of the SAP system. The file present in the AL11 folder does not contain the ‘#’ character at the end of each line, but while processing it further in the SAP system, the records shows ‘#’ at the end, due to which it cannot be processed further. There is standard SAP ABAP code and we cannot alter it to ignore this special character

Could anybody advice the possible reason and resolution for this?Could this be added by the Integration layer. 

flat file being generated 

 

Processing records at SAP

entry.title: Re: GR based invoice verification and Good receipt flag n...

entry.title: Re: Special character # -issue in the file generated
entry.content: The character is likely a LF character which is not a displayable character.

entry.title: Re: Fieldglass-Hyperscaler technical checklist
entry.content:
Hi Tina

Which type of certificates are you referring to? For browser access or system to system integrations? Please see the info below.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Customers access the SAP Fieldglass application over the Internet using the secure TLS protocol. As part of the protocol, digital certificates are used to encrypt the communication.

When SAP Fieldglass is deployed on the public cloud, the digital certificates for the server instances will be rotated every 30 days. This differs from the current implementation, where rotation was done yearly.

Browser Access

Customers who access the SAP Fieldglass application using a web browser do not require users to explicitly install SAP Fieldglass server certificates, as the browser’s certificate store will typically trust certificates issued by the “Let’s Encrypt” certificate authority. This may be dependent on IT policies / implementations, so it may be best to confirm with those teams, but action is typically not required.

System to system integration

Customers who use the system-to-system integrations (example: Web Services integrations) with SAP Fieldglass application should consult their IT departments / development teams to determine if their integration application requires the installation of SAP Fieldglass certificates to establish a trusted connection.  

SAP Fieldglass does not recommend that the customer store or install the SAP Fieldglass certificates (known as “Pinning”) because of how quickly they are rotated. If the customer chooses to store or install the SAP Fieldglass certificate, additional work is needed from the customer side to continue to access the SAP Fieldglass application without disruptions.

If the customer does not trust the refreshed SAP Fieldglass server certificate, then disruptions will occur in system-to-system integrations between the customer application and SAP Fieldglass.

entry.title: No Error for Serialized product confirming production task
entry.content:
Why system does not throw any error if no serial number is being entered for Serialized Product while confirming the production task?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No Error for Serialized product confirming production...
entry.content:
The production task has a lot of flexibility, like the user can add input products and remove existing input products as well.

When the user provides the confirmation quantity and choose enter, the system automatically picks the input products whose confirmation method is backflush. However, when it comes to serialized products, system  always considers as explicit (even if in the production model the confirmation method is maintained as backflush), as the system cannot backflush the quantities by automatically determining serial numbers.

For serialized products, quantity gets confirmed and will be updated only if user adds the serial number to the input product. In Production Task, there are cases where user does not want to utilize some of the input products and continue the process and can confirm the remaining input products quantity.

Hence, for these scenarios, system does not display any error or warning messages.

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?

Thank you!
